jobs.title: Product Manager/ Business Analyst
jobs.jd_full:
We are seeking a Business Analyst to join our team and play a pivotal role in shaping the products we build for our customers. You will be a thought leader in defining the requirements elicitation process and will be responsible with ensuring that our deliveries align with the business vision and maximise the value for the stakeholders.

Key Responsibilities

  • Define, improve, and standardise the requirements elicitation process.
  • Shape products' vision and backlogs working closely with our customers' Product Owners.
  • Write clear, testable user stories and acceptance criteria working with both internal and external technical stakeholders (Solution Architects, Software Engineers, QA).
  • Run discovery workshops with both technical and non-technical stakeholders to advance product development and identify new opportunities.
  • Contribute to winning new business by engaging with potential customers in a pre-sales capacity.
  • Coach and mentor less experienced team members.

Skills, Knowledge and Expertise

  • Previous experience as a client facing Business Analyst across diverse sized projects.
  • Confident in challenging assumptions and working with the customers’ Product Owners to define an PoC/MVP for a product.
  • Experience coaching Product Owners including defining product canvasses, building roadmaps and setting product and sprint goals.
  • Ability to effectively structure backlogs to define a product using epics and features aligning both business needs and technical architecture.
  • Ability to write clear, testable user stories using Gherkin acceptance criteria.
  • Ability to lead Stand-ups, Retrospectives, Showcases, Planning Sessions, Iteration Planning Sessions and Project Discovery Sessions, and Backlog Refinement Sessions.
  • Expert understanding of Scrum.
  • Solid experience working in an Agile team to agree and develop system/product designs based on user requirements and specify these as user stories.
  • Ability to create and sustain effective stakeholder relationships, across all levels.
  • Resilient and proactive with a strong delivery focus.
  • Strong modelling skills across the data, application and business process domains using standard modelling conventions (i.e. UML, BMPN, etc).
  • Ability to become familiar with complex business domains quickly.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication in English.

Benefits

  • Remote-first working with flexibility around client needs.
  • Private Medical Insurance.
  • Projects with global brands & public sector leaders, working on real-world impact.
  • A collaborative culture of excellence and continuous improvement.
  • Competitive salary and long-term growth opportunities.

Originally posted on Himalayas

jobs.title: Senior Project Manager ALPR
jobs.jd_full:
Join Axon and be a Force for Good.

At Axon, we’re on a mission to Protect Life. We’re explorers, pursuing society’s most critical safety and justice issues with our ecosystem of devices and cloud software. Like our products, we work better together. We connect with candor and care, seeking out diverse perspectives from our customers, communities and each other.

Life at Axon is fast-paced, challenging and meaningful. Here, you’ll take ownership and drive real change. Constantly grow as you work hard for a mission that matters at a company where you matter.

Your Impact

Own and accelerate the permitting process for Axon’s ALPR infrastructure deployments by eliminating roadblocks before they surface. This role turns complexity into clarity—guiding customers through regulatory hurdles, standardizing scalable workflows, and enabling compliant, high-velocity installations across the U.S.

What You’ll Do

Location: Remote. 30% travel required.

Must be within commutable distance to one of our Hub locations listed below

(Scottsdale AZ, Seattle Washington, Boston MA, Atlanta GA)

Reports to: Director, Professional Services

As a Sr. Project Manager at Axon, you are a national permitting authority—driving strategy, reducing risk, and steering deployment teams through the complex landscape of multi-jurisdictional approvals. You’ll work at the intersection of public safety, engineering, and local government—serving as a trusted advisor for both internal teams and external agencies.

This position demands deep regulatory knowledge, exceptional communication skills, and the ability to design scalable processes for high-volume deployments. You’ll lead engagement with Authorities Having Jurisdiction (AHJs), oversee national compliance, and de-risk field operations long before equipment hits the ground.

Key Responsibilities

  • Authority Engagement
    • Build and maintain proactive relationships with permitting and zoning officials nationwide
    • Interpret, anticipate, and influence local and regional permitting dynamics
    • Act as the regulatory liaison between Axon and AHJs
  • Strategic Permit Leadership
    • Own end-to-end permitting workflows across multiple states and municipalities
    • Design, implement, and maintain scalable tracking and reporting tools
    • Optimize permitting timelines in alignment with ALPR project schedules
  • Risk & Regulatory Navigation
    • Identify permitting barriers early and implement mitigation strategies
    • Translate code frameworks (NEC, NFPA, local zoning) into deployment-ready requirements
    • Partner with compliance and legal teams to ensure documentation and approvals are audit-ready
  • Cross-Functional Collaboration
    • Act as a core partner to engineering, deployment, legal, and customer success
    • Provide permitting forecasts and impact guidance to project teams
    • Advocate for permitting considerations during early-stage project planning

What You Bring

  • 8+ years managing infrastructure, utility, or construction permitting in enterprise or public-sector environments
  • Demonstrated expertise with NEC, NFPA, zoning, environmental regulations
  • Track record of influencing internal stakeholders and external regulators
  • Certifications such as PMP, ICC Permit Technician, OSHA 30, NFPA, LEED preferred
  • Proficiency in GIS, CAD, Asana, and permit tracking systems
  • Strong analytical, documentation, and process-building capabilities
  • Proven ability to lead in complex, multi-jurisdictional environments

Preferred Qualifications

  • Permitting & Regulatory
    • Large-scale permitting leadership across jurisdictions
    • Deep code knowledge (NEC, NFPA, zoning, environmental)
    • Public safety, DOT, or utility permitting experience preferred
  • Project & Process Management
    • Advanced risk management and issue resolution
    • Permitting workflows for high-volume infrastructure rollouts
    • Proficiency in tools like Smartsheet, Excel, and Asana
  • Stakeholder Engagement
    • Skilled communicator across technical and government audiences
    • Able to secure buy-in and resolve conflicts with AHJs
  • Cross-Functional Execution
    • Success collaborating across engineering, legal, compliance, and field teams
    • Fast-paced environment readiness and change agility
  • Tools & Systems
    • GIS, CAD, blueprint interpretation
    • Permit and document control systems

Benefits that Benefit You

  • Competitive salary and 401k with employer match
  • Discretionary paid time off
  • Paid parental leave for all
  • Medical, Dental, Vision plans
  • Fitness Programs
  • Emotional & Mental Wellness support
  • Learning & Development programs
  • And yes, we have snacks in our offices

Benefits listed herein may vary depending on the nature of your employment and the location where you work.

Don’t meet every single requirement? That’s ok. At Axon, we Aim Far. We think big with a long-term view because we want to reinvent the world to be a safer, better place. We are also committed to building diverse teams that reflect the communities we serve.

Studies have shown that women and people of color are less likely to apply to jobs unless they check every box in the job description. If you’re excited about this role and our mission to Protect Life but your experience doesn’t align perfectly with every qualification listed here, we encourage you to apply anyways. You may be just the right candidate for this or other roles.

Important Notes

The above job description is not intended as, nor should it be construed as, exhaustive of all duties, responsibilities, skills, efforts, or working conditions associated with this job. The job description may change or be supplemented at any time in accordance with business needs and conditions.

Some roles may also require legal eligibility to work in a firearms environment.

We collect personal information from applicants to evaluate candidates for employment. You may request access, deletion, or exercise other CCPA rights at axongreenhousesupport@axon.com or via our Axon Privacy Web Form. For more information, please see the Your California Privacy Rights section of our Applicant and Candidate Privacy Notice.

Axon’s mission is to Protect Life and is committed to the well-being and safety of its employees as well as Axon’s impact on the environment. All Axon employees must be aware of and committed to the appropriate environmental, health, and safety regulations, policies, and procedures. Axon employees are empowered to report safety concerns as they arise and activities potentially impacting the environment.

We are an equal opportunity employer that promotes justice, advances equity, values diversity and fosters inclusion. We’re committed to hiring the best talent — regardless of race, creed, color, ancestry, religion, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship status, marital status, disability, gender identity, genetic information, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable laws, regulations and ordinances — and empowering all of our employees so they can do their best work. If you have a disability or special need that requires assistance or accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please email recruitingops@axon.com.  Please note that this email address is for accommodation purposes only. Axon will not respond to inquiries for other purposes.

Phishing alert:  Axon will never ask you to pay for any part of the hiring process, including training, equipment, or background checks. We do not make job offers via text message, WhatsApp, or instant messaging platforms without a formal interview process.  All legitimate job openings are listed on our official careers page at https://www.axon.com/careers.  If you receive a suspicious offer or outreach from an email address that is not @axon.com, or if you are asked for sensitive personal information (bank details, Social Security Number) prematurely, please ignore the message and report it to recruitingops@axon.com.

jobs.title: National Accounts Project Manager
jobs.jd_full:
Build the Future with Us — EquipmentShare is Hiring a National Accounts Project Manager

At EquipmentShare, we’re not just filling a role — we’re assembling the best team on the planet to build something that’s never been built before. We’re on a mission to transform an industry that’s been stuck in the past by empowering contractors and communities through innovative technology, real-time support, and a team that truly cares.

We’re hiring a National Accounts Project Manager at our rental facility in Dallas, TX, and we’re looking for someone who’s ready to grow with us, bring energy and drive to their work, and help us build the future of construction. The core purpose of this position is to serve as a strategic support partner to our top National Account Managers, relieving them of administrative burdens so they can maximize focus on account growth. Responsibilities involve cross-functional collaboration with National Account Coordinators, branch personnel, Territory Account Managers, and billing teams to achieve objectives and resolve complex operational challenges.

Salary range: $65,000 to 80,000+/yr. Factors including past experience and location may affect final compensation rate. This role may qualify for an annual performance bonus. A cell phone, laptop, and work from home office set up will be provided.

Schedule: Remote position requiring 40 hours in a standard work week.

Primary Responsibilities

  • Manage and execute daily administrative tasks for National Accounts.

  • Provide comprehensive support to National Account Managers across project planning, customer agreement preparation, and execution of both.

  • Coordinate and mobilize internal resources to ensure deadlines are met.

  • Ensure clear, consistent, and timely communication with the National Account Manager.

  • Track, troubleshoot, and report all rental activity metrics, including quoting, ordering, billing, and sourcing.

  • Identify and document operational issues and inefficiencies, escalating them to the appropriate stakeholders.

  • Collaborate with internal departments to fulfill customer requests and rapidly resolve issues, supporting the National Account Manager in solution delivery.

  • Perform logistics management, including scheduling events and meetings, planning travel, and reconciling expenses.

  • Occasional travel is required for customer events, trade shows, and conferences nationwide.

Why EquipmentShare?

Because we do things differently — and we think you’ll feel it from day one. We’re a people-first company powered by cutting-edge technology. That means our proprietary T3 platform doesn’t just run our business — it also makes your job easier, safer, and more connected. Whether you’re behind the wheel, under the hood, leading a branch, or closing deals — tech supports you, and you drive us forward.

We’re a team of problem-solvers, go-getters, and builders. And we’re looking for teammates who take pride in doing meaningful work and want to be part of building something special.

Perks & Benefits

  • Monthly Family Dinner Night — We treat you and your family to dinner every month, because family comes first. (An employee favorite!) *restrictions apply

  • Competitive compensation

  • Full medical, dental, and vision coverage for full-time employees

  • Generous PTO + paid holidays

  • 401(k) + company match

  • Tool and boot reimbursements (role dependent)

  • Gym membership stipend + wellness programs (earn PTO and prizes!)

  • Company events, food truck nights, and monthly team dinners

  • 16 hours of paid volunteer time per year — give back to the community you call home

  • Career advancement, leadership training, and professional development opportunities

About You

You want to be part of a team that’s not just changing an industry for the sake of change — we’re transforming it to make it safer, more secure, and more productive. You bring grit, heart, and humility to your work, and you’re excited about the opportunity to grow within a fast-paced, mission-driven environment.

We’re looking for people who:

  • See challenges as opportunities

  • Embrace change and continuous improvement

  • Bring energy, effort, and optimism every day

Skills & Qualifications

  • Strong organizational and time-management skills in fast-paced environments

  • Ability to communicate effectively with crews, leadership, and vendors

  • Basic understanding of construction drawings and concrete work processes

  • Willingness to learn and grow within the construction materials and equipment industry

  • Self-motivated and dependable with a strong work ethic

A Workplace For All

At EquipmentShare, we believe the best solutions come from a team that reflects the world around us. Our initiative — A Workplace For All — is rooted in the belief that we must work together to solve some of the toughest problems in construction. That means attracting, developing, and retaining great people from all walks of life.

We value different backgrounds, talents, and perspectives. We want you to feel like you belong here — because you do.

EquipmentShare is an EOE M/F/D/V.

Employment is contingent on passing a background check. Additionally, some roles require passing a drug test, depending on the job responsibilities.

jobs.title: Project Manager Wealth Management Products
jobs.jd_full:
Company Description

Blend is a premier AI services provider, committed to co-creating meaningful impact for its clients through the power of data science, AI, technology, and people. With a mission to fuel bold visions, Blend tackles significant challenges by seamlessly aligning human expertise with artificial intelligence. The company is dedicated to unlocking value and fostering innovation for its clients by harnessing world-class people and data-driven strategy. We believe that the power of people and AI can have a meaningful impact on your world, creating more fulfilling work and projects for our people and clients.

Job Description

Our client, a global financial services institution, is seeking a Project Manager to support a high-performing Wealth Management marketing organization.  This team is responsible for delivering integrated marketing initiatives across a portfolio of client segments and financial products.

This role is ideal for a senior-level program manager who thrives in complex, highly regulated environments and brings a strong combination of marketing domain knowledge, governance expertise, and cross-functional leadership.  You will play a critical role in driving execution, establishing structure, and ensuring alignment across multiple concurrent marketing initiatives.

The Details:

  • Location: Remote within the U.S.
  • Duration: 12-month consulting role with potential for extension (must be able to work on Blend’s W2 without requiring employer sponsorship either now or in the future)
  • Benefits: We do offer benefits to our full-time consultants, including Health, Vision, Dental, 401K plan, Life Insurance, Employee Assistance Programs, and an incredibly supportive team cheering you on!

What you’ll do:

The Project Manager will serve as a central driver of execution and governance across a portfolio of strategic marketing initiatives.  This role will partner closely with marketing leaders, strategy teams, and cross-functional stakeholders to ensure programs are delivered on time, within scope, and aligned to business objectives.

This is a highly visible role requiring strong organizational discipline, stakeholder management, and the ability to build and enforce governance frameworks in a matrixed environment.

Program Governance & Structure

  • Establish and maintain program governance frameworks, including project charters, RACI matrices, decision logs, and escalation protocols.
  • Ensure consistent oversight and alignment across multiple marketing workstreams.
  • Drive accountability across stakeholders without direct authority.

Roadmap & Execution Management

  • Develop and manage integrated marketing roadmaps, including timelines, milestones, dependencies, and deliverables.
  • Track progress against strategic priorities to ensure on-time, on-budget delivery.
  • Identify risks, issues, and blockers, and proactively implement mitigation strategies.

Cross-Functional Coordination

  • Partner with marketing managers, strategy leads, and execution teams to ensure seamless delivery across initiatives.
  • Coordinate workflows across business, creative, legal, compliance, and operational teams.
  • Manage approval processes and ensure all deliverables meet required standards and checkpoints.

Program Operations & Administration

  • Lead day-to-day program management activities, including scheduling and facilitating meetings, capturing action items, and tracking follow-ups.
  • Maintain detailed documentation to ensure transparency, accountability, and audit readiness.

Stakeholder Communication & Reporting

  • Deliver regular status updates and executive-level reporting, including progress summaries, risks, and key decisions.
  • Communicate effectively across all levels of the organization, including senior leadership.
  • Ensure alignment across stakeholders in a highly matrixed environment.

Qualifications

Education & Experience

  • Bachelor’s degree required; advanced degree preferred.
  • 8+ years of experience in program or project management within complex, enterprise environments.
  • Proven experience managing multiple large-scale, cross-functional initiatives simultaneously.
  • Prior experience supporting marketing organizations or campaign delivery teams.
  • Background in financial services or wealth management

Technical & Functional Skills

  • Deep expertise in program governance, process design, and execution management.
  • Strong understanding of marketing campaign lifecycles, approval workflows, and go-to-market execution.
  • Experience managing legal and compliance approval processes within regulated environments.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (Excel, PowerPoint) for reporting and executive communication.

Soft Skills

  • Exceptional communication and stakeholder management skills.
  • Ability to influence and drive alignment across teams without direct authority.
  • Highly organized with strong attention to detail and follow-through.
  • Proactive, solutions-oriented mindset with strong problem-solving capabilities.
  • Comfortable operating in fast-paced, highly matrixed environments with competing priorities.

Additional Information

The starting pay range for this role is $55-60 per hour. Actual compensation within the range will be dependent on several factors including but not limited to relevant experience, skills, certifications, training, and location. It is not typical for an individual to be hired at or near the top of the range and determining factors for compensation are considered for each individual circumstance. BLEND360 also offers a competitive benefits program to meet the health and financial well-being of our team and their families. You can look forward to a range of benefits including medical, dental, vision, 401K, PTO, commuter benefits, spending accounts, life insurance, disability coverage, and EAPs.

A diverse workforce is a strong workforce

To deliver growth at BLEND360 and for our clients, we believe as a Talent Solutions Company, we have a responsibility and unique opportunity to positively impact the workforce. Diversity has played a critical role in our history, our growth, and continues to have a profound impact on our success.  We are determined to have equality in the workplace, within our team and as an extension of our clients’ team.

BLEND360 is an equal opportunity employer.

jobs.title: Senior Product Manager
jobs.jd_full:
About the Company

At Torc, we have always believed that autonomous vehicle technology will transform how we travel, move freight, and do business. A leader in autonomous driving since 2007, Torc has spent over a decade commercializing our solutions with experienced partners. Now a part of the Daimler family, we are focused solely on developing software for automated trucks to transform how the world moves freight. Join us and catapult your career with the company that helped pioneer autonomous technology, and the first AV software company with the vision to partner directly with a truck manufacturer.

Meet the Team:

This team is looking for someone with combined technical and product management experience to define and drive the next generation of on-road autonomous driving technology, meeting customer needs as effectively and efficiently as possible. Works closely with cross-functional teams to define the capabilities and features that will unlock autonomous driving. In addition, they will deliver actionable product definitions and respective roadmaps to achieve product milestones, integrate plans with organizational leaders, obtain feedback from customers and stakeholders, and maintain a backlog of work that balances customer requirements with technological feasibility and company objectives. They will also own the product definition, scope, prioritization, and cross-functional collaboration to enable the product development plan of their specific area as well as serve as a subject matter expert. Finally, this position will work closely with potential customers, engineering, technical partners, strategy, marketing, and sales to ensure that the product meets the needs of Torc and future customers.

What You’ll D o:

  • Drive the product vision internally by developing high-quality product definition, scoping, and prioritization.

  • Drive the development of product features and functionality by liaising between the engineering team and relevant stakeholders. Gather user requirements, build product requirements, and communicate with engineering and other stakeholders at all levels of planning and execution.

  • Define clear, testable, and concise high-quality product requirements, success metrics and prioritization based on customer feedback, market research, and business priorities, and effectively communicate them to the relevant TPM and engineering teams.

  • Develop and propose process improvement initiatives to ensure cross-functional alignment between teams and projects. Support the process rollout and iterative cycle for continuous improvement.

  • Actively participate in regular planning exercises to support product prioritization across the company.

  • Analyze and understand customer, commercial, and technical constraints and tradeoffs by collaborating with engineering, commercial, legal, safety and regulatory to inform product roadmaps and backlogs. Recommend path forward with agreement from senior managers.

  • Define, analyze, communicate, and continuously update key product plans and metrics to ensure priorities remain up to date.

  • Develop and execute compelling customer demos and customer meeting outcomes.

  • Collaborate, research, review competitor communications, and analyze and compare competing products to ensure Torc maintains the best products available.

  • Help set, track, and achieve goals aligned with company-wide vision, goals, and initiatives.

  • Champion customer-focused development across Torc and support the business to embrace customer-centric work culture.

  • Act as a role model for Torc’s Values.

What you’ll need to Succeed:

  • Demonstrates competences and technical proficiencies typically acquired through:

    • BS+ in business, engineering, computer science or related field +10 years of experience.
    • MS+ 7+ years of experience.
    • PHD+ 5+ years of experience.

  • Considered highly skilled and proficient in discipline; conducts complex, important work under minimal supervision and with a wide latitude for independent judgment.

  • Demonstrated experience with multiple products, not just a particular area of interest or expertise. Should be willing to be PM for a wide range of topics.

  • Experience with Autonomous driving systems, Advanced driver-assistance systems and/or related robotics technologies.

  • Understanding of automotive systems, including software, hardware and systems integration, verification and validation, testing, safety & compliance.

  • Understanding of applicable safety standards and regulations related to autonomous vehicles (e.g. ISO26262, SOTIF, FuSa, UL4600, AVSC, etc).

Bonus Points!

  • Experience with Operational Design Domain definition.
  • Experience working JAMA/Cameo or related tools.
  • Experience in defining fault and failure tolerant safety critical systems.

Perks of Being a Full-time Torc’r

Torc cares about our team members and we strive to provide benefits and resources to support their health, work/life balance, and future. Our culture is collaborative, energetic, and team focused. Torc offers:

  • A competitive compensation package that includes a bonus component and stock options
  • 100% paid medical, dental, and vision premiums for full-time employees
  • 401K plan with a 6% employer match
  • Flexibility in schedule and generous paid vacation (available immediately after start date)
  • Company-wide holiday office closures
  • AD+D and Life Insurance

At Torc, we’re committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace. We celebrate the uniqueness of our Torc’rs and do not discriminate based on race, religion, color, national origin, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, veteran status, or disabilities. Even if you don’t meet 100% of the qualifications listed for this opportunity, we encourage you to apply.

Our compensation reflects the cost of labor across several geographic markets. Pay is based on a number of factors and may vary depending on job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Torc’s total compensation package will also include our corporate bonus and stock option plan. Dependent on the position offered, sign-on payments, relocation, and other forms of compensation may be provided as part of a total compensation package, in addition to a full range of medical, financial, and/or other benefits.

Job ID: R-102699

Hiring Range for Job Opening

US Pay Range

$160,800—$193,000 USD

jobs.title: Project Manager CORE
jobs.jd_full:
We are looking for a high-impact Executive Assistant/Project Manager to act as a strategic partner and collaborator with our C-level team. This role is designed for a “force multiplier”, someone who can seamlessly blend high-level executive support with Project Management.

If you are a process-oriented professional who thrives on keeping things organized, are excellent at closing loops, and ensuring no project milestone is missed, you’ll fit right in. We need someone proactive, tech-savvy, and ready to navigate high-level operations in a fast-paced environment.

WHAT YOU WILL BE DOING

Executive Support & Strategic Coordination

  • Calendar & Meeting Lifecycle: Manage complex C-suite calendar, prioritizing high-value engagements and ensuring leadership is briefed and prepared for every meeting.
  • Communication Hub: Act as the primary gatekeeper for incoming calls and emails, filtering priorities and gathering critical data from stakeholders to keep workflows moving.
  • Administrative Excellence: Handle the “gears” of the executive office - from complex document management and filing to coordinating signatures and maintaining strict confidentiality.
  • Maintain strict confidentiality with sensitive client and business information while demonstrating professionalism.
  • Communicate with clients regarding questions, updates, and relevant matters related to their cases.

Project Management & Operations

  • Project Oversight: Own the lifecycle of internal projects, tracking milestones, managing deadlines, and ensuring team accountability from planning to execution.
  • Workflow Optimization: Constantly look for ways to streamline “how we work” within Google Workspace and our internal project tools.
  • Research & Organization: Conduct deep-dive research to support internal operations and strategic initiatives, presenting findings in organized, actionable formats.

WHAT WE WANT YOU TO HAVE

  • At least three (3) years of solid experience as an Executive Assistant or Project Manager, specifically supporting teams or managing operational projects.
  • Project & Sales Mindset: Strong time-management skills with the ability to organize, track, and drive multiple projects and sales tasks to completion simultaneously.
  • Technical Proficiency: Power user of Google Workspace; ability to quickly master CRM software, AI tools, and Project Management tools (e.g., Asana, Monday, or ClickUp).
  • Adaptability: A “wearer of many hats” who can pivot from administrative filing to high-level problem-solving without missing a beat.
  • Communication: Exceptional verbal and written skills, with the ability to adjust tone and style for diverse audiences—from interns to C-suite.
  • Traits: Meticulous attention to detail, process-oriented, and a proactive problem-solver who anticipates needs before they are even voiced.
  • Reliability: Absolute discretion with sensitive business information and a commitment to punctual, consistent performance.

Minimum Technical and Work Environment Requirements:

  • Internet Connection:

    • Primary internet connection with a minimum speed of 15 Mbps.
    • Backup internet connection with at least 10 Mbps.
    • Backup connection must be capable of supporting work during a power outage.

  • Primary Device:

    • Desktop or laptop equipped with at least:

      • Intel Core i5 (8th generation or newer), Intel Core i3 (10th generation or newer), AMD Ryzen 5, or an equivalent processor.
      • A minimum of 8 GB RAM.

  • Backup Device:

    • Must meet or exceed the performance of an Intel Core i3 processor.
    • Must be functional during power interruptions.

  • A functioning webcam.

  • A noise-canceling USB headset.

  • A quiet, dedicated home office space.

  • Peripherals and Workspace: A smartphone for communication and verification purposes.

  • Join Our Dynamic Team: Experience our fun, inclusive, innovative culture that values your unique contributions and supports your professional growth.

  • Embrace the Opportunities: Seize daily chances to learn, innovate, and excel. Make a real impact in your field.

  • Limitless Career Growth: Unlock a world of possibilities and resources to propel your career forward.

  • Fast-Paced Thrills: Thrive in a high-energy, engaging atmosphere. Embrace challenges and reap stimulating rewards.

  • Flexibility, Your Way: Embrace the freedom to work from home or any location of your choice. Create your ideal work environment.

  • Work-Life Balance at Its Best: Say goodbye to stressful commutes and hello to quality time with loved ones. Achieve a healthy work-life integration to perform at your best.
